description: Fix all remaining gaps between the current docs site and the RetroUI NeoBrutalism aesthetic. The theme toggle, search button, mobile menu, sidebar, social icons, and several CSS details do not match RetroUI. Every visible UI element must have RetroUI treatment: border-2, hard offset shadows, Archivo Black headings, press-down hover effects, sharp corners.

userStories.title: Style ThemeSelect (light/dark toggle) with RetroUI NeoBrutalism
userStories.description: As a user, I want the theme toggle dropdown to have RetroUI styling with thick borders, hard shadows, and press effects
acceptanceCriteria: A ThemeSelect.astro override exists in src/overrides/ and is registered in astro.config.mjs
The theme toggle button has border-2 border-black (matching RetroUI Button outline variant)
The toggle button has shadow-sm (2px 2px 0 0 var(--border)) and hover:shadow-none
The toggle button has hover:translate-y-0.5 active:translate-y-1 for press effect
The dropdown panel (if visible) has border-2 border-black shadow-md bg-card
Dropdown items have hover:bg-primary hover:text-primary-foreground transitions
The toggle works correctly in both light and dark mode
userStories.notes: Override ThemeSelect via components: { ThemeSelect: './src/overrides/ThemeSelect.astro' } in astro.config.mjs. Import the default: import DefaultThemeSelect from '@astrojs/starlight/components/ThemeSelect.astro'. Wrap and apply styles via :global() selectors targeting the select button and dropdown. Starlight's ThemeSelect renders a <starlight-theme-select> custom element with a <select> inside it. Target: starlight-theme-select select { border: 2px solid var(--border); border-radius: 0; box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); padding: 0.25rem 0.5rem; font-family: var(--font-head); cursor: pointer; } and on hover/focus: box-shadow: none; transform: translateY(1px).

userStories.title: Style search button with RetroUI NeoBrutalism
userStories.description: As a user, I want the search button to have RetroUI styling with thick borders and hard shadow
acceptanceCriteria: The Pagefind search trigger button has border-2 using var(--border)
The button has hard offset shadow from RetroUI shadow scale (shadow-sm)
The button has hover:shadow-none and hover:translate-y-0.5 press effect
The search modal/overlay has border-2 and shadow-lg
Search input inside modal has border-2 border-black matching RetroUI Input classes: 'px-4 py-2 w-full rounded border-2 shadow-md transition focus:outline-hidden focus:shadow-xs'
Styling applied via CSS in global.css targeting Starlight/Pagefind selectors

userStories.title: Style mobile menu toggle with RetroUI NeoBrutalism
userStories.description: As a user on mobile, I want the hamburger menu button to have RetroUI styling
acceptanceCriteria: The mobile menu toggle button has border-2 using var(--border)
The button has hard offset shadow (shadow-sm)
The button has hover:shadow-none and press effect
Styling applied via CSS in global.css targeting Starlight's mobile menu button

userStories.title: Style sidebar with RetroUI typography and section borders
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar to feel like RetroUI with bold section headers and styled group separators
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar section/group headers (Guide, Plugins, Reference) use font-head (Archivo Black)
Sidebar group headers have a bottom border using var(--border) for visual separation
Active sidebar link has border-l-2 border-primary AND bg-accent/20 for stronger visibility
Sidebar overall has a right border: border-r-2 border-border on desktop
Styling applied via CSS in global.css

userStories.title: Style social icon links (GitHub) with RetroUI NeoBrutalism
userStories.description: As a user, I want social links in the header to have RetroUI button styling
acceptanceCriteria: Social icon links have border-2 using var(--border)
Styling applied via CSS in global.css

userStories.title: Fix body colors and add RetroUI scrollbar + text-outlined utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the body element to use RetroUI theme tokens and have RetroUI's custom scrollbar and text-outlined utility available
acceptanceCriteria: body has background: var(--background) and color: var(--foreground) set explicitly
Custom scrollbar styles from RetroUI source are added (scrollbar-width, scrollbar-color, webkit-scrollbar rules)
The .text-outlined utility class is available (from RetroUI global.css lines 383-390)
Light mode inline code uses var(--muted) instead of hardcoded #f0f0f0
Header shadow updated to use RetroUI shadow scale value (0 3px 0 0 var(--border))

userStories.title: Style table of contents (right sidebar) with RetroUI NeoBrutalism
userStories.description: As a user, I want the table of contents panel to match the RetroUI aesthetic
acceptanceCriteria: TOC panel has a left border: border-l-2 border-border on desktop
TOC heading 'On this page' uses font-head (Archivo Black)
TOC active link has text-primary color
Styling applied via CSS in global.css
